name: Steve Swallow
abstract: Steve Swallow (born October 4, 1940) is an American jazz bassist and composer, known for his collaborations with Jimmy Giuffre, Gary Burton, and Carla Bley. He was one of the first jazz double bassists to switch entirely to electric bass guitar.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Gary Burton Quartet
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Gary Burton Quartet
Newport Jazz Festival: Solo Flight (The Virtuosity of the Jazz Musician)
JVC Jazz Festival: Lee Konitz 80th Birthday Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: the bass player that was the most successful there was a guy named Steve Swallow. We had a very good quartet together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve known Steve Swallow most of my life and he used to commission me to write a piece... Steve suggested that I had this thing already written... And we practiced it, we rehearsed it out in California I remember, and recorded it in New York... with Bob Moses, Larry Coryell, Steve Swallow and Gary.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: When Chuck Israels left the band, Steve Swallow, who had sat in with us several times... Then he joined us. He was – that particular version of the band was now Steve Swallow, still Joe Hunt, George Russell, and we added Eric Dolphy.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I grabbed Steve Swallow... we’d do a little date together... we would go up there... carry the bass up there and do this concert... I’ve been working more with Steve now again after — we had our group together for a while
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Middleburgh, New York, May 11, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: play with Roy Haynes and Steve Swallow... I met Steve earlier in that playing in Pete LaRoca’s quartet... One of the first bands I put together... with Steve Swallow
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I had met Swallow through Moses... me and Swallow go back with Moses to before Pete... with Chick and him and Swallow, the rhythm section, it was unbelievable.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
